subject: A Level Mathematics
chapter_name: Kinematics of motion in a straight line

note_data.title: Kinematics of motion in a straight line
keyPoints: **Displacement** is the distance moved in a specified direction from a fixed origin, measured in metres (m)
**Velocity** is the rate of change of displacement with respect to time, measured in m/s
**Acceleration** is the rate of change of velocity with respect to time, measured in m/s²
The **suvat equations** apply only when acceleration is constant throughout the motion
Choose a **positive direction** at the start and maintain this convention throughout the problem
When an object returns to its starting position, the **displacement is zero** but distance travelled is not
At the **highest point** of vertical motion, velocity equals zero but acceleration remains -g
The **gradient** of a displacement-time graph gives velocity at that instant
The **area under** a velocity-time graph gives the displacement in that time interval

definitions.definition: The distance moved in a specified direction from a fixed point, measured as a vector quantity
The rate of change of displacement with respect to time, measured as a vector quantity
The rate of change of velocity with respect to time, measured as a vector quantity
Motion where the acceleration remains constant in magnitude and direction

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Use suvat equations showing clear substitution and working
Show numerical substitution into correct formula with units in final answer

modelAnswer.question: A cricket ball is thrown vertically upward from ground level with initial velocity 20 m/s. Find the maximum height reached and the time taken to return to ground level. (Take g = 10 m/s²)
modelAnswer.answer: Taking upward as positive direction, u = 20 m/s, a = -10 m/s², v = 0 at maximum height. Using v² = u² + 2as: 0² = 20² + 2(-10)s, 0 = 400 - 20s, s = 20 m. Maximum height = 20 m. For time to return to ground: s = 0, using s = ut + ½at²: 0 = 20t + ½(-10)t², 0 = 20t - 5t², 0 = t(20 - 5t), t = 0 or t = 4. Time to return = 4 s.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct choice of positive direction
Mark 2: Correct use of v² = u² + 2as
Mark 4: Correct use of s = ut + ½at²

commonMistakes: Never confuse displacement with distance - displacement can be negative but distance is always positive
Students often forget to define positive direction clearly at the start of the problem
Never use suvat equations when acceleration is not constant
Students incorrectly think acceleration is zero at the highest point of projectile motion
Always include units in final answers - marks are lost for missing units
examinerTips: This topic appears in every Paper 4 and frequently in Paper 2
Always draw a labelled diagram showing positive direction for vertical motion problems
Show clear substitution into suvat equations for method marks
Check answers make physical sense - negative time or impossible speeds indicate errors
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Vertical line showing initial position, positive direction (usually upward), initial velocity vector, and acceleration vector (downward for gravity). Label all known values clearly.
